proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen vs not.
Mean pfs_months is higher in patients receiving treatment_palbociclib than in those not receiving it.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab vs not.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_olaparib vs not.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan vs not.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab vs not.

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen-treated mean PFS 4.723 vs 4.680 untreated; difference +0.043 mo, t-test p=0.077.
Palbociclib-treated mean PFS 5.408 vs 4.310 untreated; difference +1.098 mo, t-test p<1e-300.
Trastuzumab-treated mean PFS 4.645 vs 4.705 untreated; difference -0.060 mo, t-test p=0.029.
Olaparib-treated mean PFS 4.660 vs 4.696 untreated; difference -0.036 mo, t-test p=0.330.
Sacituzumab-treated mean PFS 4.686 vs 4.694 untreated; difference -0.007 mo, t-test p=0.846.
Pembrolizumab-treated mean PFS 4.630 vs 4.704 untreated; difference -0.074 mo, t-test p=0.018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months is lower in stage_iv=1 vs stage_iv=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months is lower in patients with has_brain_mets=1 vs has_brain_mets=0.
Mean pfs_months is higher in er_positive=1 vs er_positive=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months is higher in pr_positive=1 vs pr_positive=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months is lower in her2_positive=1 vs her2_positive=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months is lower in pik3ca_mutation=1 vs pik3ca_mutation=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months declines with rising ecog_ps level (0,1,2).
Mean pfs_months differs between brca1_mutation carriers and non-carriers.
Mean pfs_months differs between brca2_mutation carriers and non-carriers.
Mean pfs_months is higher in her2_low=1 vs her2_low=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months differs between node_positive=1 and node_positive=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months differs between postmenopausal=1 and postmenopausal=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female=1 and sex_female=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: stage_iv=1: 3.617 mo vs stage_iv=0: 5.153 mo; diff -1.536 mo, p<1e-300.
Brain mets: 3.750 vs 4.800 mo; diff -1.050 mo, p=1.5e-178.
PIK3CA-mut: 4.328 vs WT: 4.887 mo; diff -0.559 mo, p=6.1e-144.
HER2-low+: 4.765 vs not: 4.650 mo; diff +0.114 mo, p=1.0e-6.
Postmenopausal: 4.684 vs pre: 4.707 mo; diff -0.023 mo, p=0.311.
Female: 4.686 vs male: 4.699 mo; diff -0.013 mo, p=0.562.

proposed_hypotheses.text: pfs_months is positively correlated with age_years.
pfs_months is negatively correlated with ki67_pct.
pfs_months is positively correlated with albumin_g_dl.
pfs_months is negatively correlated with weight_loss_pct_6mo.
pfs_months is negatively correlated with ldh_u_l.

pfs_months is correlated with crp_mg_l, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl, or tumor_size_cm.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.7015, p<1e-300. Older patients have substantially higher PFS in this dataset.

analyses.code: stats.pearsonr for the remaining lab variables
analyses.result_summary: tumor_size_cm r=+0.0004 p=0.93; crp r=-0.003 p=0.46; nlr r=+0.003 p=0.54; hemoglobin r=-0.005 p=0.30; alk_phos r=-0.013 p=3e-3; ast r=-0.003 p=0.56; alt r=-0.004 p=0.42; bilirubin r=-0.009 p=0.046; creatinine r=-0.004 p=0.38; bun r=-0.001 p=0.80; sodium r=-0.002 p=0.67; potassium r=+0.010 p=0.025; calcium r=-0.013 p=4e-3. All correlations are very small even when significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of treatment_tamoxifen on pfs_months is more positive in er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months is more positive in er_positive=1 than in er_positive=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_palbociclib on pfs_months is more negative (smaller benefit) in her2_positive=1 than in her2_positive=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is more positive in her2_positive=1 than in her2_positive=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_trastuzumab on pfs_months is more positive in her2_low=1 than in her2_low=0 patients.
The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is more positive in brca1_mutation=1 patients.
The effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months is more positive in brca2_mutation=1 patients.
The effect of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan on pfs_months is more positive in her2_low=1 patients.
The effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on pfs_months is more positive in er_positive=0 patients (i.e., negative interaction with er_positive).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction tam x er_positive: coef=-0.058, p=0.270. Tamoxifen effect +0.023 mo in ER+, +0.081 in ER-. No ER+-specific benefit.
Interaction pal x er_positive: coef=+1.594, p=9.3e-229. Palbociclib effect +1.577 mo in ER+ vs -0.017 mo in ER-. Strong ER+-specific benefit.
Interaction pal x her2_positive: coef=-1.364, p=2.2e-118. Palbociclib effect -0.014 mo in HER2+ vs +1.350 mo in HER2-.
Interaction trast x her2_positive: coef=-0.028, p=0.69. Trastuzumab: -0.079 mo in HER2+, -0.051 mo in HER2-. No HER2+-specific benefit.
Interaction trast x her2_low: coef=+0.068, p=0.241. No HER2-low benefit.
Interaction ola x brca1: coef=+0.446, p=0.066. Effect +0.398 in BRCA1+ vs -0.047 in BRCA1-. Borderline.
Interaction ola x brca2: coef=+0.340, p=0.163. Effect +0.295 in BRCA2+ vs -0.045 in BRCA2-. NS.
Interaction sac x her2_low: coef=-0.038, p=0.626. No HER2-low-specific benefit.
Interaction pem x er_positive: coef=-0.132, p=0.050. Effect -0.111 in ER+, +0.020 in ER-. Borderline; pembrolizumab benefit (if any) is NOT clearly in ER- patients either.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with hr_pos_her2_neg = (er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1) AND her2_positive=0, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months relative to no palbociclib; outside this subgroup it does not.
Among patients with er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months; outside it does not.
Among patients with brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1 (any BRCA), treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup it does not.
Among patients with er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0 (TNBC), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months.
Among patients with er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0 (TNBC), treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months.

analyses.code: stratify by HR+/HER2-; ttest treated vs not within stratum; OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within HR+/HER2- (n=30444): palbociclib +1.811 mo (p<1e-300). Outside: -0.009 mo (p=0.78). Interaction coef +1.820, p<1e-300.
Within ER+/HER2- (n=28589): palbociclib +1.924 mo (p<1e-300). Outside: -0.006 mo (p=0.85). Interaction coef +1.930, p<1e-300.
Within BRCA1/2 carriers (n=2499): olaparib +0.346 mo (p=0.041). Outside: -0.056 mo (p=0.143). Interaction coef +0.402, p=0.021.
Within TNBC (n=10496): sacituzumab -0.085 mo (p=0.22). Outside: +0.019 mo (p=0.66). Interaction NS.
Within TNBC: pembrolizumab +0.072 mo (p=0.22, NS). Outside: -0.114 mo (p=0.0017). Interaction p=0.015. Pembrolizumab is null in TNBC and slightly negative outside; no positive subgroup found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In an OLS model adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, biomarkers, labs, and all six treatments, treatment_palbociclib has a positive coefficient on pfs_months while the other treatments do not.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + pr_positive + her2_positive + her2_low + brca1_mutation + brca2_mutation + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct + tumor_size_cm + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + weight_loss_pct_6mo + crp_mg_l + nlr + hemoglobin_g_dl + treatment_*')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment coefficients: palbociclib +1.102 (p<1e-300, sig); tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.49); trastuzumab -0.009 (p=0.46); olaparib -0.026 (p=0.12); sacituzumab +0.033 (p=0.05); pembrolizumab -0.020 (p=0.17). Strong adjusted prognostic effects: age +0.176/yr, ecog -1.17/level, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.98, ER+ +0.53, HER2+ -0.47, PIK3CA -0.58, ki67 -0.019/unit, albumin +0.48/g/dL, weight_loss -0.077/% per unit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each treatment, the strongest interaction modifier among {stage_iv, has_brain_mets, node_positive, postmenopausal, er_positive, pr_positive, her2_positive, her2_low, brca1_mutation, brca2_mutation, pik3ca_mutation, age_years, ki67_pct, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, weight_loss_pct_6mo, nlr, hemoglobin_g_dl} reveals a clinically meaningful subgroup with differential treatment effect.

analyses.code: Loop: for each treatment, run OLS pfs_months ~ t + b + t:b for each candidate b; rank by p_int.
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen: top hit stage_iv (int coef -0.118, p=0.021); no large interaction. Palbociclib: dominated by pik3ca_mutation (coef -1.62, p=4e-255), er_positive (+1.59, p=9e-229), ki67_pct(z) (-0.64, p=1e-174), her2_positive (-1.36, p=2e-118), pr_positive (+1.09, p=3e-117), her2_low (+0.42, p=9e-19). Trastuzumab: top hit ki67_pct(z) +0.053 p=0.052; no real subgroup. Olaparib: top hits brca1 (+0.45, p=0.066) and brca2 (+0.34, p=0.16) — borderline. Sacituzumab: top albumin(z) +0.093 p=0.013. Pembrolizumab: top er_positive -0.13 p=0.050. Only palbociclib shows large interaction effects with any feature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months substantially; outside this subgroup it has no effect.
Adding ki67_pct<20 to the er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 subgroup further enriches the palbociclib effect.
Adding pr_positive=1 to the er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<20 subgroup does not meaningfully change the palbociclib effect.

analyses.result_summary: Within sgA (n=18681): palbociclib +2.908 mo (p<1e-300). Outside: +0.003 mo (p=0.91). Interaction coef +2.905, p<1e-300.
Within sgB=ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/ki67<20 (n=14174): palbociclib +3.845 mo (p<1e-300). Outside: -0.002 mo (p=0.94). Interaction coef +3.846, p<1e-300.

Within sgC (n=12070): palbociclib +3.900 mo. Outside: +0.195 (p=8e-16) — outside includes the PR-/sgB patients who still benefit. Adding PR+ does not exclusively localize the effect; PR is not a necessary modifier on top of ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<20.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA1/2 carriers, the effect of treatment_olaparib on pfs_months differs by additional modifiers (er_positive, pr_positive, her2_positive, stage_iv, postmenopausal).
Treatment_pembrolizumab benefits a subgroup of TNBC defined by ECOG 0/1, high CRP, or high NLR.
Treatment_trastuzumab benefits a subgroup of HER2+ patients defined by ER status, stage, or other co-variates.

analyses.code: Within BRCA+ (n=2499) test interaction olaparib x feature
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA+, the only borderline modifier is er_positive (interaction coef -0.685, p=0.084) and pr_positive (-0.65, p=0.074). Olaparib effect +0.144 in ER+ vs +0.829 in ER- carriers (suggests effect is concentrated in BRCA+/ER- patients).

analyses.code: Stratify TNBC by ECOG<=1, CRP>10, NLR>4
analyses.result_summary: TNBC + ECOG<=1: pembrolizumab +0.114 mo (p=0.063, n=1335 vs 7553). TNBC + CRP>10: -0.117 (p=0.40). TNBC + NLR>4: -0.073 (p=0.50). No clearly positive pembrolizumab subgroup identified.

analyses.code: Within HER2+ test trastuzumab x feature interactions
analyses.result_summary: Within HER2+, no interaction modifier reaches p<0.20 (er_positive p=0.84, pr_positive p=0.70, stage_iv p=0.56, brain_mets p=0.29, postmenopausal p=0.41, node_positive p=0.78). Trastuzumab does not appear to benefit any HER2+ subgroup. HER2+/ER- subgroup test: -0.063 mo (p=0.55).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup it does not.
Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND her2_positive=0, treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months; outside this subgroup it does not.

analyses.code: Stratify on BRCA-any & ER-; ttest and OLS interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA+/ER- (n=750): olaparib +0.829 mo (p=0.0012, n=55+695). Outside: -0.048 (p=0.20). Interaction coef +0.877, p=0.013.
Within BRCA+/HER2- (n=2023): olaparib +0.321 mo (p=0.090). Outside: -0.051 (p=0.18). Interaction coef +0.371, p=0.054. Borderline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0 AND stage_iv=1 (advanced TNBC), treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC + stage IV (n=3140): sacituzumab -0.262 mo (p=0.018, n=333+2807) — actually slightly negative. Outside: +0.018 (p=0.64). No positive sacituzumab subgroup identified; this finding is opposite to the hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=0 (premenopausal HR+), treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months relative to no tamoxifen.
Within er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 (HR+/HER2-), treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/premenopausal (n=13805): tamoxifen +0.044 mo (p=0.38). Outside: +0.042 (p=0.14). Interaction NS (coef +0.002, p=0.97). No premenopausal-specific tamoxifen benefit.
Within ER+/HER2- (n=28589): tamoxifen +0.025 mo (p=0.48). Outside: +0.053 (p=0.097). Interaction NS (coef -0.028, p=0.57). Tamoxifen has no detectable subgroup benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 2-feature subgroup search will identify additional treatment-effect heterogeneity beyond the canonical pairs already tested, especially for trastuzumab, sacituzumab, pembrolizumab, and tamoxifen.

analyses.code: For each treatment, loop over all (feature1, value1)x(feature2, value2) combinations where subgroup n>=200 and treated count>=30, fit OLS pfs_months ~ t + s + t:s, retain combos with int p<0.001.
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib (top results): er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 (int +1.93, p=0); er_positive=1 & pik3ca_mutation=0 (int +2.37, p=0); er_positive=1 & ki67_high=0 (int +2.08); pik3ca_mutation=0 & ki67_high=0 (int +2.19); brca2_mutation=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0 (int +1.58, p=4e-246). Olaparib: only node_positive=1 & brca_any=1 (int +0.79, p=8.5e-4) — survives at p<0.001 once but small n. Tamoxifen, trastuzumab, sacituzumab, pembrolizumab: NO 2-feature subgroup at p<0.001. Only palbociclib (and weakly olaparib) shows true heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<20, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~3.8 months; outside this subgroup it has no effect.

Within er_positive=1 AND pr_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<20, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by ~3.9 months — adding PR+ does not narrow the effect further.

analyses.code: Stratify on er_positive=1 & her2_positive=0 & pik3ca_mutation=0 & ki67_pct<20 (sgB)
analyses.result_summary: Within sgB (n=14174): palbociclib +3.845 mo (p<1e-300). Outside: -0.002 mo (p=0.94). Interaction coef +3.846, p<1e-300. Confirms the 4-feature subgroup definition.

Within sgC (n=15916): palbociclib +3.900 mo. Outside (n=34084): +0.195 (p=8e-16) — the outside still includes PR- members of sgB who DO benefit. PR+ is correlated with ER+ but is NOT a necessary additional modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The Ki67 cutoff for palbociclib benefit within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT is approximately a sharp threshold near Ki67=14, with full benefit (~+5 months) for Ki67<14 and essentially zero benefit for Ki67>=14.

analyses.code: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT loop ki67 cutoff c=8..21; compute palbociclib effect on each side; OLS interaction on the cut-defined subgroup.
analyses.result_summary: Sharp cutoff near ki67=14: with cut<14 the low-Ki67 palbociclib effect is +4.94 mo (n=10752) and the high-Ki67 effect is +0.07 mo (n=7929); interaction coef peaks at +4.93 (p<1e-300). Cuts >=14 contaminate the high-Ki67 stratum with non-responders. Cuts <14 lose few responders. Best-supported cutoff = ki67_pct<14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0, treatment_olaparib still increases pfs_months.
Within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0 (BRCA+ TNBC), treatment_olaparib increases pfs_months.

analyses.code: Stratify BRCA-any AND ER- AND HER2-
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA+/ER-/HER2- (n=599): olaparib +0.736 mo (p=0.010, n=43+556). Interaction p=0.049, coef +0.781.
Within BRCA+/TNBC (n=498): olaparib +0.751 mo (p=0.022, n=35+463). Interaction p=0.070 (borderline), coef +0.795.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous-modifier scans for trastuzumab, pembrolizumab, sacituzumab, and tamoxifen will not reveal any clinically meaningful predictive biomarker that defines a positive treatment-effect subgroup.

analyses.code: For each (treatment, continuous feature) standardize the feature and fit OLS pfs_months ~ t + fz + t:fz
analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab: best continuous modifier tumor_size_cm(z) +0.066/z (p=0.018) — small. Pembrolizumab: alkaline_phosphatase(z) -0.058/z (p=0.060). Sacituzumab: albumin(z) +0.093/z (p=0.013). Tamoxifen: ki67(z) +0.038/z (p=0.118). All effect sizes are small (<0.1 mo per SD) and none survive multiple-testing thresholds; confirming the hypothesis that no strong predictive biomarker exists for these four treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within her2_positive=1 AND ki67_pct>=20 (aggressive HER2+), treatment_trastuzumab increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within HER2+/Ki67>=20 (n=2125): trastuzumab -0.063 mo (p=0.58, n=420+1705). Within HER2+/Ki67<20 (n=47875): -0.061 (p=0.033). No HER2+ subgroup of any kind shows a trastuzumab benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC AND ki67_pct>=20 (highly proliferative TNBC), treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months.
Within TNBC AND nlr<3 (lymphocyte-rich TNBC), treatment_pembrolizumab increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC/Ki67>=20 (n=2576): pembrolizumab -0.040 (p=0.73, n=418+2158). No benefit.
Within TNBC/NLR<3 (n=5256): pembrolizumab +0.006 (p=0.94, n=796+4460). No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC AND her2_low=1, treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within TNBC/HER2-low (n=4737): sacituzumab -0.072 mo (p=0.49, n=492+4245). No benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within er_positive=1 AND pr_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0, treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months.
Within er_positive=1 AND postmenopausal=1, treatment_tamoxifen increases pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/postmenopausal (n=21081): tamoxifen +0.010 mo (p=0.81, n=6344+14737). Outside: +0.067 (p=0.029). No tamoxifen benefit anywhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ki67_pct, and pik3ca_mutation, the palbociclib effect within er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<14 remains large and significantly positive.

analyses.code: smf.ols within sgB' (ki67<14): pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_palbociclib
analyses.result_summary: Within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<14, adjusted palbociclib coef = +4.977 mo, p<1e-300. Effect is robust to adjustment for all major prognostic covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age_years, ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, albumin_g_dl, weight_loss_pct_6mo, ki67_pct, her2_positive, and pik3ca_mutation, the olaparib effect within (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) AND er_positive=0 remains positive.

analyses.code: smf.ols within BRCA+/ER- subgroup with prognostic covariates plus treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA+/ER- (n=750), the unadjusted olaparib effect of +0.829 mo collapses to -0.003 mo (p=0.92) after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage_iv, brain mets, HER2, PIK3CA, ki67, albumin, weight_loss. Olaparib-treated patients in this subgroup are 3.1 yr older and have lower stage IV and brain-mets rates, so the unadjusted effect is largely confounded by baseline prognostic differences. The apparent BRCA+/ER- benefit is NOT robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a large fully-adjusted multivariable OLS model, the only treatment whose marginal effect on pfs_months is materially positive is treatment_palbociclib.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + er_positive + pr_positive + her2_positive + pik3ca_mutation + ki67_pct + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_*')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted main effects: tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.50); palbociclib +1.102 (p<1e-300, sig); trastuzumab -0.010 (p=0.46); olaparib -0.026 (p=0.13); sacituzumab +0.033 (p=0.05); pembrolizumab -0.020 (p=0.15). Palbociclib is the only treatment with a robust adjusted PFS benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within their canonical biomarker-defined subgroups (tamoxifen-ER+, trastuzumab-HER2+, sacituzumab-TNBC, pembrolizumab-TNBC), each of these four treatments has an adjusted PFS benefit.

Within er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 AND ki67_pct<14, treatment_palbociclib increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months (signed positive). Outside this subgroup, treatment_palbociclib has no PFS effect. This is the final best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for pfs_months.

analyses.code: Within each canonical subgroup, smf.ols pfs_months ~ prognostic covariates + treatment indicator
analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen in ER+: adjusted coef +0.001, p=0.94. Trastuzumab in HER2+: -0.0004, p=0.95. Pembrolizumab in TNBC: +0.008, p=0.16. Sacituzumab in TNBC: +0.006, p=0.32. None of these four treatments shows an adjusted PFS benefit within its canonical biomarker subgroup. Hypothesis refuted for all four.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs_months ~ age_years + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + treatment_palbociclib within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-WT/Ki67<14; full-cohort interaction confirmation.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib coef within sgB' = +4.977 mo, p<1e-300. Outside the subgroup, full-cohort adjusted palbociclib coef is +0.008 mo (p=4.5e-4 from interaction model), with the additional in-subgroup gain of +4.969 mo (p<1e-300). Full-cohort total effect in subgroup: +4.977. Strongly supported.
